title: Reading and Use of English – Part 3
description: For questions 17–24, read the text below. Use the word given in CAPITALS at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line.

content.text: The (0) INVENTION (INVENT) of the printing press by Johannes Gutenberg in the 15th century was a (17) REVOLUTIONARY (REVOLUTION) moment. Before this, books were (18) PAINFULLY (PAIN) slow to produce, as they had to be copied by hand. This (19) LIMITATION (LIMIT) meant that knowledge was restricted to a wealthy elite. However, the new technology made books more (20) AFFORDABLE (AFFORD) for the general public. As a result, (21) LITERACY (LITERATE) rates began to rise (22) DRAMATICALLY (DRAMA) across Europe. The (23) AVAILABILITY (AVAILABLE) of information encouraged people to think for themselves and challenge (24) TRADITIONAL (TRADITION) beliefs. This period of (25) ENLIGHTENMENT (LIGHT) paved the way for the scientific and cultural advancements of the Renaissance. Today, the digital age has further transformed how we consume information, but the impact of the printed word remains a fundamental part of our history, shaping the way we learn and communicate in a modern society that values access to knowledge above all else.

example.text: The (0) INVENTION (INVENT) of the printing press...
example.explanation: Noun required after the definite article; INVENT → INVENTION.

questions.explanation: Adjective required to modify 'moment'; REVOLUTION → REVOLUTIONARY.
Adverb required to modify the adjective 'slow'; PAIN → PAINFULLY.
Noun required as the subject of the clause; LITERATE → LITERACY.
Adverb required to modify the verb 'rise'; DRAMA → DRAMATICALLY.
Noun required after the definite article; AVAILABLE → AVAILABILITY.
Adjective required to modify 'beliefs'; TRADITION → TRADITIONAL.
